title: Braiden Shaw says AI is this generation's 'one window' to build wealth — but warns time to capitalize is running out
excerpt: The investor says the AI infrastructure boom is where most people should be investing their money — but warns the window to act is shrinking.
content_html:
Everybody has a story about money they missed out on. Amazon shares nobody bought at $18 in 1997 or Google stock that sold for $85 in 2004 and seemed too expensive at the time. In hindsight it's free money, but at the time it looked like a risk you didn't want to take.

Amplifi LLC Chief Executive Officer Braiden Shaw has built a video around that feeling. Shaw, who played Division I basketball at Brigham Young University and now has 1.5 million Instagram followers, says every generation gets "exactly one window" to build real wealth.

Must Read

  • Jeff Bezos backs a platform that lets anyone invest in rental homes for as little as $100 — 6 ways to build wealth like a landlord without actually being one

  • Dave Ramsey warns nearly 50% of Americans are making 1 big Social Security mistake — here's what it is and 3 simple steps to fix it ASAP

  • Millionaires under 43 hold only 25% of their wealth in stocks. Here's where their money is actually going

The 1920s had the stock market opening up to regular people. The late 1990s had the internet. He says in both cases, the window opens, money moves and most people only realize what's happened after it's over.

He believes the window is open right now thanks to artificial intelligence. Here's a breakdown of what he's seeing and what it means for you.

The evidence that something is happening

Shaw's case starts with what he thinks people misread last time. The internet didn't make people rich because they used the websites. It made the people who understood the physical infrastructure being built underneath it rich. He says AI has the same shape — most people see a chatbot they type into, but the money goes into data centers, semiconductors, cooling systems, transmission lines and the power to run all of it.

He's right about the scale and he points to Morgan Stanley to prove it. In October 2025, the firm's Global Investment Committee said that the market has become so concentrated in the "Magnificent 7" tech giants and the data center ecosystem around them, that this bull run now rests largely on AI spending. The buildout Shaw is pointing at is holding up the whole market.

The timing is where it gets tricky. That same note said the boom "may be closer to the seventh inning than the first or second," which is a way of saying this big spending wave is already well underway, not just starting.

Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Chief Investment Officer Lisa Shalett told Fortune that people confuse AI adoption, which she puts in the first inning, with the buildout itself — which has "been going full-out since 2022." Shaw doesn't hide from that. He cites the seventh-inning line in his own video and says it doesn't mean the opportunity is over, just that the easy phase (buy anything connected to AI and watch it run) is running out.

Shaw doesn't hide from that. sentences.en: Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Chief Investment Officer Lisa Shalett told Fortune that people confuse AI adoption, which she puts in the first inning, with the buildout itself — which has "been going full-out since 2022."
sentences.zh: 摩根士丹利财富管理公司首席投资官丽莎·沙莱特 (Lisa Shalett) 告诉《财富》杂志，人们将人工智能的采用（她在第一局中提出）与扩建本身混为一谈——后者“自 2022 年以来一直在全面发展”。

sentences.en: Shaw doesn't hide from that.
sentences.zh: 肖并没有隐瞒这一点。

sentences.en: He cites the seventh-inning line in his own video and says it doesn't mean the opportunity is over, just that the easy phase (buy anything connected to AI and watch it run) is running out.
sentences.zh: 他在自己的视频中引用了第七局的台词，并表示这并不意味着机会已经结束，只是简单阶段（购买任何与人工智能相关的东西并观看其运行）已经结束。

content_html_zh:
每个人都有一个关于他们错过的钱的故事。 1997 年，没人以 18 美元的价格购买亚马逊股票；2004 年，谷歌股票以 85 美元的价格出售，当时看来太贵了。 事后看来，这是免费的钱，但当时看起来你不想冒这个风险。

Amplifi LLC 首席执行官 Braiden Shaw 围绕这种感觉制作了一个视频。 肖曾在杨百翰大学打过一级篮球比赛，现在在 Instagram 上拥有 150 万粉丝，他说每一代人都有“一个窗口”来积累真正的财富。

必读

  • 杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 支持一个平台，让任何人都可以以低至 100 美元的价格投资出租房屋——无需真正成为房东即可像房东一样积累财富的 6 种方法
  • Dave Ramsey 警告说，近 50% 的美国人在社会保障方面犯了 1 个重大错误——这就是它的本质，以及尽快修复它的 3 个简单步骤
  • 43岁以下的百万富翁仅将其财富的25%投资于股票。 这就是他们的钱实际去向的地方

20 年代，股票市场向普通民众开放。 20 世纪 90 年代末出现了互联网。 他说，在这两种情况下，窗口都会打开，资金会流动，大多数人只有在事情结束后才意识到发生了什么。

他相信，由于人工智能，窗口现在已经打开。 以下是他所看到的内容及其对您的意义的详细说明。

某事正在发生的证据

肖的案例始于他认为人们上次误读的内容。 互联网并没有让人们变得富有，因为他们使用了网站。 它让那些了解其地下基础设施建设的人们变得富有。 他说人工智能具有相同的形状——大多数人看到的是他们输入的聊天机器人，但资金投入到数据中心、半导体、冷却系统、传输线和运行所有这些的电力上。

他对规模的看法是正确的，并指出摩根士丹利可以证明这一点。 2025 年 10 月，该公司的全球投资委员会表示，市场已经变得如此集中于“Magnificent 7”科技巨头及其周围的数据中心生态系统，以至于这次牛市现在主要依赖于人工智能支出。 肖所指的扩张正在支撑整个市场。

时机是变得棘手的地方。 同一篇说明称，繁荣“可能比第一局或第二局更接近第七局”，这从某种意义上说，这一大笔支出浪潮已经在顺利进行，而不仅仅是开始。

摩根士丹利财富管理公司首席投资官丽莎·沙莱特 (Lisa Shalett) 告诉《财富》杂志，人们将人工智能的采用（她在第一局中提出）与扩建本身混为一谈——后者“自 2022 年以来一直在全面发展”。 肖并没有隐瞒这一点。 他在自己的视频中引用了第七局的台词，并表示这并不意味着机会已经结束，只是简单阶段（购买任何与人工智能相关的东西并观看其运行）已经结束。
